{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Prototyping and Test Engineer",
  "description": "Arridex is a trusted leader in industrial innovation, delivering tailored solutions that enhance resilience and sustainable development across critical sectors. With a legacy spanning more than two decades in West Africa&rsquo;s industrial landscape, we have evolved from a trusted asset integrity solutions provider into an industrial technology partner, offering solutions that address critical operational challenges.

Our deep understanding of local challenges affecting industrial operations, coupled with our commitment to technological advancement and innovation, allows us to offer a lot of value to our stakeholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Prototyping and Test Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Overview


	We are seeking a Prototyping and Test Engineer to own the physical build loop, additive manufacturing operations, and environmental and functional testing of our hardware platforms.
	This role sits at the intersection of making and validating: it is the role that turns design concepts into testable objects in days, and that produces the verification evidence a defence or industrial buyer will require.
	Speed and rigour, in equal measure.


Key Responsibilities


	Collaborate on the operation and programming of additive manufacturing equipment to produce prototypes and production-representative parts
	Run the rapid iteration loop, taking designs from digital file to physical test article and feeding findings back into the design cycle
	Own the test and verification programme for each platform, developing test plans, executing tests, and producing test reports
	Develop and maintain environmental and qualification test protocols suited to maritime and defence operating requirements
	Identify design flaws through physical testing before they reach production, reducing cost and programme risk
	Work across the team to ensure prototyping priorities align with the product roadmap


Requirements


	Hands-on experience operating industrial additive manufacturing equipment, including FFF, SLS, or similar processes
	Practical hardware prototyping and fabrication background with a portfolio of physical products built and tested
	Experience developing and executing structured test programmes for mechanical, electronic, or integrated systems
	Systematic approach to documenting test results and translating findings into design recommendations
	Nigerian nationals strongly preferred


Preferred Background:


	Experience with maritime, defence, or safety-critical environmental qualification testing
	Familiarity with MIL-STD, IP ratings, or equivalent environmental standards
	Background in post-processing and finishing of additive manufactured parts for functional and aesthetic requirements.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: bakpanenang@arridex.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-07-27",

Prototyping and Test Engineer Job at Arridex




        
    

        
        Arridex is a trusted leader in industrial innovation, delivering tailored solutions that enhance resilience and sustainable development across critical sectors. With a legacy spanning more than two decades in West Africa’s industrial landscape, we have evolved from a trusted asset integrity solutions provider into an industrial technology partner, offering solutions that address critical operational challenges.

Our deep understanding of local challenges affecting industrial operations, coupled with our commitment to technological advancement and innovation, allows us to offer a lot of value to our stakeholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Prototyping and Test Engineer
     

            
    



Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Overview


	We are seeking a Prototyping and Test Engineer to own the physical build loop, additive manufacturing operations, and environmental and functional testing of our hardware platforms.
	This role sits at the intersection of making and validating: it is the role that turns design concepts into testable objects in days, and that produces the verification evidence a defence or industrial buyer will require.
	Speed and rigour, in equal measure.


Key Responsibilities


	Collaborate on the operation and programming of additive manufacturing equipment to produce prototypes and production-representative parts
	Run the rapid iteration loop, taking designs from digital file to physical test article and feeding findings back into the design cycle
	Own the test and verification programme for each platform, developing test plans, executing tests, and producing test reports
	Develop and maintain environmental and qualification test protocols suited to maritime and defence operating requirements
	Identify design flaws through physical testing before they reach production, reducing cost and programme risk
	Work across the team to ensure prototyping priorities align with the product roadmap


Requirements


	Hands-on experience operating industrial additive manufacturing equipment, including FFF, SLS, or similar processes
	Practical hardware prototyping and fabrication background with a portfolio of physical products built and tested
	Experience developing and executing structured test programmes for mechanical, electronic, or integrated systems
	Systematic approach to documenting test results and translating findings into design recommendations
	Nigerian nationals strongly preferred


Preferred Background:


	Experience with maritime, defence, or safety-critical environmental qualification testing
	Familiarity with MIL-STD, IP ratings, or equivalent environmental standards
	Background in post-processing and finishing of additive manufactured parts for functional and aesthetic requirements.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Social Media Manager",
  "description": "Madop Associates - Our client is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Manager&nbsp;

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos (Hybrid)
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Schedule: 3 Days on site

Job Summary


	We are recruitinga creative and strategic Social Media Manager to oversee our social media presence, create engaging content, grow our online community, and enhance brand visibility for our client.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for developing and executing social media strategies that drive engagement, increase followers, and support business objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement social media strategies aligned with business goals.
	Create, schedule, and publish engaging content across social media platforms.
	Manage and grow the company&#39;s social media accounts, including Facebook, Instagram, X (Twitter), LinkedIn, TikTok, and other relevant platforms.
	Monitor social media trends and recommend innovative content ideas.
	Engage with followers, respond to comments and messages, and foster community growth.
	Track, analyze, and report on social media performance metrics.
	Collaborate with the marketing and design teams to create compelling campaigns.
	Maintain brand consistency across all social media channels.


Requirements


	Proven experience in social media management or digital marketing.
	Strong content creation, copywriting, and storytelling skills.
	Knowledge of social media analytics and management tools.
	Basic graphic design and video editing skills are an added advantage.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Creative mindset with attention to detail.


Salary
N150,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hr@madopassociates.com using the Job Position as subject of the email.

Note: For more inforamtion, Whatsapp us: 07078585903.",

Social Media Manager Job at Madop Associates




        
    

        
        Madop Associates - Our client is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Manager 

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos (Hybrid)
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Schedule: 3 Days on site

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are recruitinga creative and strategic Social Media Manager to oversee our social media presence, create engaging content, grow our online community, and enhance brand visibility for our client.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for developing and executing social media strategies that drive engagement, increase followers, and support business objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and implement social media strategies aligned with business goals.
	Create, schedule, and publish engaging content across social media platforms.
	Manage and grow the company's social media accounts, including Facebook, Instagram, X (Twitter), LinkedIn, TikTok, and other relevant platforms.
	Monitor social media trends and recommend innovative content ideas.
	Engage with followers, respond to comments and messages, and foster community growth.
	Track, analyze, and report on social media performance metrics.
	Collaborate with the marketing and design teams to create compelling campaigns.
	Maintain brand consistency across all social media channels.


Requirements


	Proven experience in social media management or digital marketing.
	Strong content creation, copywriting, and storytelling skills.
	Knowledge of social media analytics and management tools.
	Basic graphic design and video editing skills are an added advantage.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Creative mindset with attention to detail.


Salary
N150,000 per month. 

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of the email.

Note: For more inforamtion, Whatsapp us: 07078585903.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Front Desk Receptionist Job at Events Architects Limited




        
    

        
        Events Architects started with a simple belief: events should be more than logistics and decoration. They should be architected, designed with intention, built on strategy, and executed with precision. In 2005, we introduced Event Architecture to Nigeria: a methodology that treats event design like building a structure. You start with a strong foundation (understanding the brand and audience), develop a creative concept, plan every detail, and manage the technical execution. Then you bring it all to life.
     

            
    



Twenty years later, we’ve refined this approach across corporate galas, product launches, diplomatic gatherings, cultural celebrations, and intimate weddings. We’ve worked with global brands like Google, McKinsey, Microsoft, and L’Oréal. We’ve created platforms like Q5 Party, TGIF x The Big Birthday, and EPIC Conference. We’ve built The Wedding Company and Events Architects Academy.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Front Desk Receptionist
Job Location: Ajah / Abraham Adesanya - Lagos

Requirements


	Excellent communication and customer service skills.
	Good interpersonal abilities.
	Basic computer knowledge is an added advantage.


Salary
N100,000 per month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV and applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the email subject.

Note: For more enquires, Call / WhatsApp: 08116602831.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Madop Associates - Our client is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a dynamic and results-driven Marketer to promote the products/services, generate leads, and drive business growth for our client.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for developing marketing strategies, engaging potential customers, and increasing brand awareness.


Responsibilities


	Develop and implement effective marketing strategies.
	Identify and pursue new business opportunities.
	Promote products and services to prospective clients.
	Build and maintain strong customer relationships.
	Conduct market research to identify customer needs and industry trends.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND, HND, or Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Previous experience in marketing, sales, or customer service is an advantage.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong negotiation and persuasion abilities.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	Goal-oriented with a passion for achieving targets.


Salary
N100,000 - N120,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover letter to: hr@madopassociates.com using the Job Position as subject of the email.

Note: For more inforamtion, Whatsapp us: 07078585903 .",

Marketer Job at Madop Associates




        
    

        
        Madop Associates - Our client is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a dynamic and results-driven Marketer to promote the products/services, generate leads, and drive business growth for our client.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for developing marketing strategies, engaging potential customers, and increasing brand awareness.


Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Develop and implement effective marketing strategies.
	Identify and pursue new business opportunities.
	Promote products and services to prospective clients.
	Build and maintain strong customer relationships.
	Conduct market research to identify customer needs and industry trends.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND, HND, or Bachelor's Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Previous experience in marketing, sales, or customer service is an advantage.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong negotiation and persuasion abilities.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	Goal-oriented with a passion for achieving targets.


Salary
N100,000 - N120,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of the email.

Note: For more inforamtion, Whatsapp us: 07078585903 .

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Representative",
  "description": "Paga is a licensed financial services company and a leading payments company in Nigeria with a massive transformative purpose - to make it simple for one billion people to access and use money. It was founded on the simple belief that the ubiquity of mobile phones can be leveraged in building an ecosystem that enables people to digitally send and receive money and creating simple financial access for everyone.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Representative

Job Location: Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time&nbsp;

Job Description


	The Customer Service Representative is responsible for answering incoming calls from various customers in a pleasant and professional manner.
	Understand different Paga products and services and effectively answer customers appropriately.
	The individual will be expected to fill out customer complaint forms and follow the complaint resolution process and assist all Paga customers.
	Also perform miscellaneous tasks as directed by the Customer Care Manager or Supervisor.


Responsibilities


	Deal directly with customers via telephone, chat and face-to-face
	Monitor call tracking for responses/feedback from other units for call returns done in a timely fashion
	Document all calls with regards to clients enquiries accurately for reporting purpose
	Respond promptly to customers enquiries
	Handle and resolve customer complaints
	Direct requests and unsolved issues to designated resource
	Weekly Agent outlet visitations
	Maintain and hand-in weekly report of activities
	Contribute to team efforts by accomplishing related results as needed
	Follow-up on customer interactions within a 24-hour period &nbsp;
	Resolve customers&rsquo; complaints and enquiries regarding products and services.
	Other roles as assigned by the call centre supervisor.


Requirements


	University Degree or HND equivalent
	1-2 years of work experience in a call centre and customer service environment
	Knowledge of customer service principles and practices
	Ability to recognize and manage assertive and irate customers
	Good product knowledge
	Strong customer interface
	Computer literate with ability to capture data accurately and timely
	Must have completed the mandatory NYSC.


Key Competencies:


	Strong communication skills &ndash; verbal and written
	Interpersonal skills
	Customer focused skills
	Listening skills
	Telephone skills
	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Flexibility.

Customer Service Representative Job at Paga Limited




        
    

        
        Paga is a licensed financial services company and a leading payments company in Nigeria with a massive transformative purpose - to make it simple for one billion people to access and use money. It was founded on the simple belief that the ubiquity of mobile phones can be leveraged in building an ecosystem that enables people to digitally send and receive money and creating simple financial access for everyone.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Representative

Job Location: Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time 

Job Description
     

            
    




	The Customer Service Representative is responsible for answering incoming calls from various customers in a pleasant and professional manner.
	Understand different Paga products and services and effectively answer customers appropriately.
	The individual will be expected to fill out customer complaint forms and follow the complaint resolution process and assist all Paga customers.
	Also perform miscellaneous tasks as directed by the Customer Care Manager or Supervisor.


Responsibilities


	Deal directly with customers via telephone, chat and face-to-face
	Monitor call tracking for responses/feedback from other units for call returns done in a timely fashion
	Document all calls with regards to clients enquiries accurately for reporting purpose
	Respond promptly to customers enquiries
	Handle and resolve customer complaints
	Direct requests and unsolved issues to designated resource
	Weekly Agent outlet visitations
	Maintain and hand-in weekly report of activities
	Contribute to team efforts by accomplishing related results as needed
	Follow-up on customer interactions within a 24-hour period  
	Resolve customers’ complaints and enquiries regarding products and services.
	Other roles as assigned by the call centre supervisor.


Requirements


	University Degree or HND equivalent
	1-2 years of work experience in a call centre and customer service environment
	Knowledge of customer service principles and practices
	Ability to recognize and manage assertive and irate customers
	Good product knowledge
	Strong customer interface
	Computer literate with ability to capture data accurately and timely
	Must have completed the mandatory NYSC.


Key Competencies:


	Strong communication skills – verbal and written
	Interpersonal skills
	Customer focused skills
	Listening skills
	Telephone skills
	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Flexibility.
